message:
fix(fnp-ufunc): clone integer sidecar during CoW itemset so original view stays intact

`UFuncArrayView::itemset_cow()` broke copy-on-write semantics for arrays with
an integer sidecar (the parallel I64/U64 buffer we keep to preserve exact
integer values that would otherwise be lossy in the f64 main buffer).

The CoW logic correctly cloned the main `buffer` into a new Arc<RwLock<_>>
before mutating it, but it left `integer_sidecar` pointing at the same Arc
the original view shared. Any mutation that went through the sidecar on the
new view then mutated the original view's sidecar as well — CoW for the
floats, shared-state for the ints. The end result was that two views that
appeared independent would disagree about which buffer held authoritative
integer values.

Clone the sidecar the same way: read-lock it, `.clone()` the inner
`IntegerSidecar`, and wrap it in a fresh `Arc<RwLock<_>>` that replaces
`self.integer_sidecar`. Only run the clone when a sidecar exists (so
float-only arrays pay nothing). The existing "shared view: read lock
poisoned" error pattern is reused for the sidecar's poisoning case under
a slightly different message.

Regression test `test_itemset_cow_clones_sidecar` constructs an I64 array,
takes a shared view, mutates it via `itemset_cow`, and then verifies that
the *original* array's sidecar still holds the pre-mutation value (1)
while the main buffer also still reports 1.0 at that index. Fails before
the fix, passes after.

Also drops trailing whitespace on two lines inside the polyfit helper that
clippy fmt flagged in the same area.
